channel.title: Schulich Engineering Competition (SEC) on Engineering Students' Society U of C
channel.description: Recent content in Schulich Engineering Competition (SEC) on Engineering Students' Society U of C
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2023 Engineering Students Society University of Calgary. All Rights Reserved

item.title: Communications
item.description: Teams of 1-2 students are asked to pitch a creative solution for a complex problem. The presentation should include social and environmental impacts, and be created beforehand. Judging is based on overall presentation, verbal skills, and comprehension of the subject.

item.title: Competitive Programming
item.description: This competition challenges teams of 2-4 to produce a piece of industry-quality software with all the proper user and administrative documents. The teams will use their software development skills, their technical writing abilities, and their project management skills to design a solution to a problem.

item.title: Consulting
item.description: Teams of 4 students are given seven hours to devise an innovative solution to a real-world problem. Teams are expected to develop holistic solutions that consider the economic, social, and environmental impacts, then present their solutions to a panel of judges familiar with the relevant industry.

item.title: Impromptu Debate
item.description: In debate, teams of 2 students compete to convince judges of their assigned stance on a controversial engineering subject. Competitors are judged on their ability to present persuasive, well-constructed arguments.

item.title: Innovative Design.md
item.description: Innovative design is a platform for teams of 1 to 4 students to pitch the findings of research they conducted over the last year.

item.title: Junior Design
item.description: Teams of 2 - 4 students in 1st or 2nd year collaborate to solve a technical problem by designing and building a prototype. Past competitions have included designing bridges, towers, catapults or water filters from simple materials.

item.title: Senior Design
item.description: Teams of 2 - 4 students in 3rd or 4th year collaborate to solve a complex engineering problem by designing and building a working prototype. Past competitions have included designing minesweepers, automated/controlled water pollution collectors, and hill climbers.
